name: midnight straddle: reported_at as the true instant breaks an equal-validity tie across calendar days
description: Two correcting notes about Tomas's SAME-validity employer (valid_from 2026-06-01 for both) are captured on opposite sides of local midnight in different offsets. Applied in scenario order: note 1 = Initech, captured 2026-06-10T00:10:00-04:00 (true instant 04:10Z); note 2 = Globex, captured 2026-06-09T23:50:00-06:00 (true instant 05:50Z). In wall-clock terms note 2 ('23:50 on the 9th') looks earlier than note 1 ('00:10 on the 10th'), but the TRUE instants reverse that: note 1 is 04:10Z and note 2 is 05:50Z, so note 2 is the genuinely LATER report. reported_at is the absolute instant (docs/reference/ANALYSIS.md 'reported_at = note capture time, client-side with timezone'), and supersession._validity tie-breaks equal valid_from by reported_at. So Globex (note 2, later instant) wins the >= test and SUPERSEDES the active Initech (note 1) — a fact_conflict is filed. The adversarial trap is the naive-local-day reading where note 2 looks earlier; pins that the tie-break uses the absolute instant, so the later-instant Globex becomes active even though its local wall-clock date is the 9th. Lever B (ENTITY_GRAPH_INGEST_V2 §5): this clean state/relationship supersession now supersedes SILENTLY with retained history — no fact_conflict card is filed.

steps.body: Tomas started at Initech on June 1st.
Correction from a minute ago: Tomas actually started at Globex on June 1st.
